item.title: Afrim Kuçi
item.description: Afrim Kuçi (19 May 1970 – 9 October 2022) was a Kosovo Albanian footballer who played as a defender and defensive midfielder.[1]

item.title: Train Collectors Association
item.description: The Train Collectors Association (TCA) is an international non-profit organization of people who operate and collect toy trains, toy train accessories, toy train books, toy train paper, and anything else rail transport related. TCA was founded in October 1954 in Yardley, Pennsylvania and is currently headquartered in Strasburg, Pennsylvania. The National Toy Train Museum affiliated with TCA is included in the "List of museums in Pennsylvania".

item.title: Ðe lónlí blú bojs
item.description: Ðe lónlí blú bojs (phonetically, "The Lonely Blue Boys") were an Icelandic folk music band created in 1974, and lasting until 1976. Their most popular album was the Icelandic Gold Record "Stuð, stuð, stuð".[1] They only sang in Icelandic and were never popular abroad. Its members were:

item.title: Roine Karlsson
item.description: Roine Sven Torbjörn Karlsson (12 November 1943 – 17 February 2013) was a Swedish race walker. He competed in the 20 km event at the 1964 Summer Olympics where he placed 20th.[1][2] Aged 20, he was the youngest Swedish track and field athlete at those games.[3]

item.title: 1964–65 Chicago Black Hawks season
item.description: The 1964–65 Chicago Black Hawks season was the Hawks' 39th season in the NHL, and followed a second-place finish in 1963–64. Chicago won a team record of 36 games and also set a club record with 84 points. The Hawks defeated the Detroit Red Wings in 7 games in the NHL semifinals but fell to the Montreal Canadiens in the Stanley Cup Finals in another hard-fought 7-game series.[1]
